J.reasoning: Since the minimum effort determines the reward and I announced 0, choosing 0 minimizes my cost while still aligning with the expected lowest contribution, ensuring I don't lose money.

Announcing 0 also aligns with incentivizing others to lower their effort, potentially reducing the minimum further, though in this case I expect others to hold it at 1. Matching the lowest possible contribution minimizes cost while not dragging down the group reward further.

However, since my payoff depends on the minimum effort and I pay a cost for my own effort, reducing my effort to 0 maximizes my payout if others are expected to exert more effort. To avoid unnecessary cost, I choose 0 effort.

But since I announced 0 and anticipate others may still contribute higher, choosing 0 maximizes my personal payoff if the actual minimum effort ends up being 0 regardless. Given the incentive to minimize my own cost while relying on others' higher efforts, choosing 0 effort maximizes my individual payout under strategic free-riding.
